facts: Six key players (Lucas Hernandez, Warren Zaïre-Emery, Désiré Doué, Bradley Barcola, Ousmane Dembélé, William Saliba) are exempted from the initial training camp due to club commitments.
These six players are expected to be rested for the first friendly match against Ivory Coast on June 4th.
The full squad is anticipated to be available for the second friendly match against Republic of Ireland on June 8th.
Maxence Lacroix and Jean-Philippe Mateta will join the squad later after their club commitments.
Neymar is injured with a second-grade calf lesion and will miss upcoming friendly matches against Panama and Egypt.
Neymar has fifteen days to prove his fitness for the World Cup or will be sent home.
Joao Pedro could replace Neymar in the World Cup squad if he does not recover in time.
Neymar has not played for Brazil since October 2023 due to a cruciate ligament rupture.
Captain Lionel Messi is in the World Cup squad despite a recent alert for a left hamstring issue.
Midfielders Franco Mastantuono and Giovani Lo Celso are notably absent from the World Cup squad.
